title: DATA STRUCTURES AND ALGORITHMS(DSA) COURSE
description: This workshop aimed to provide insights into fundamental data structures, algorithm design, and their applications.
fullDescription: The Data Structures and Algorithms (DSA) course, starting on January 21, spans over three months and is meticulously designed for beginners aiming to excel in technical interviews. Conducted online, this comprehensive program offers a robust curriculum covering essential concepts in Java and C++. With a registration fee of ₹300, participants are provided with a thorough grounding in DSA, guided by expert instructor Abhinav Kumar from Skylark Drones. His extensive experience in data structures and algorithms ensures that learners receive top-notch education and practical insights. Sudeep and Harsha, as event coordinators, have played pivotal roles in orchestrating this course, seamlessly ensuring its success. Each week of the course delves into specific topics, beginning with foundational concepts such as Time Complexity, Pointers, and Searching Techniques. As the course progresses, participants explore a variety of critical subjects including Arrays, Strings, Sorting Techniques, Linked Lists, Stacks, Queues, Recursion, and Trees. Advanced topics such as Heap Tree, Hashing, Graphs, Dynamic Programming, Greedy Algorithms, Backtracking, and Bit Manipulation are also comprehensively covered. The final weeks include mock interviews and reviews, ensuring that participants are well-prepared for real-world technical challenges. The teaching methodology employed in the course is highly interactive and practical. Weekly lectures are designed to be comprehensive, fostering an engaging learning environment. Hands-on coding problems and practical exercises help reinforce theoretical knowledge. Participants have access to valuable resources including lecture notes, reference guides, and practice problems, enhancing their learning experience. Weekly Q&A sessions provide an opportunity for interactive discussions, allowing learners to clarify doubts and deepen their understanding. The positive feedback from participants highlights the effectiveness of this approach, appreciating the course structure and the instructor’s expertise.

title: CareerCraft
description: This workshop aimed to give insights about interview preparation and internship opportunities
fullDescription: The workshop on interview preparation, networking, personal branding, and finding the right internship opportunity was meticulously curated by Ms. Madhura Kawadiwale, an esteemed interview preparation coach and soft-skills trainer, held from 6:00 p.m. to 7:30 p.m. With a wealth of experience in talent development, Ms. Madhura delved into comprehensive strategies tailored to elevate participants' career prospects. The session commenced with a deep dive into interview preparation, encompassing techniques to articulate strengths, handle behavioral questions, and demonstrate alignment with organizational values. Participants gained invaluable insights into crafting compelling resumes, optimizing LinkedIn profiles for professional networking, and leveraging social media platforms to enhance personal branding. Throughout the workshop, Ms. Madhura seamlessly integrated practical exercises and role-playing scenarios, enabling participants to refine their communication skills, build confidence, and project a strong professional image. Discussions on effective networking strategies, including informational interviews and leveraging alumni networks, empowered participants to expand their professional connections and access hidden job markets. Furthermore, Ms. Madhura provided guidance on cultivating a distinctive personal brand, emphasizing authenticity, consistency, and storytelling to resonate with potential employers and stakeholders. As the session concluded, Ms. Madhura underscored the importance of continuous learning and self-improvement in navigating today's dynamic job market. Participants left the workshop equipped with actionable strategies, renewed confidence, and a clear roadmap to pursue internship opportunities aligned with their career aspirations. The event's success was attributed to Ms. Madhura's expertise, engaging delivery style, and the collaborative efforts of the organizing team, ensuring a transformative learning experience for all attendees.
outcomes: The workshop provided invaluable insights into interview preparation, networking, and personal branding. Participants gained practical strategies and renewed confidence to navigate the job market effectively. Ms. Madhura's expertise and engaging teaching style ensured a transformative learning experience for all attendees

title: GRE-Demystified
description: The objective of the GRE Exam Preparation Webinar was to equip prospective test-takers with comprehensive strategies and practical insights to excel in the GRE, enhancing their readiness for graduate admissions.
fullDescription: The GRE Exam Preparation Webinar, held online and led by Rohan Lakhne, was a highly anticipated event that drew the attention of 65 prospective GRE test-takers. With over a decade of experience in training students for GRE, GMAT, and SAT exams, Rohan Lakhne, the Head of Learning and Development at AECC, brought invaluable insights and practical knowledge to the session. His expertise and interactive teaching style kept the audience engaged throughout the two-hour seminar, providing a comprehensive overview of the GRE exam structure and its significance in graduate admissions. The webinar was meticulously structured to cover all critical aspects of the GRE exam, beginning with an introduction to the test's format and importance. Each exam section was explored in depth: Verbal Reasoning, Quantitative Reasoning, and Analytical Writing. Rohan shared effective strategies for tackling reading comprehension, text completion, and sentence equivalence in the Verbal section. In Quantitative Reasoning, participants learned techniques for problem-solving, data interpretation, and quantitative comparisons. The Analytical Writing segment focused on crafting compelling essays, providing participants with tips to excel in both issue and argument tasks. Additionally, Rohan discussed time management techniques, study schedules, and the importance of practice tests, which are crucial for efficient preparation. The seminar concluded with an interactive Q&A session, allowing participants to address their specific queries and concerns. This personalized interaction was highly appreciated, as reflected in the positive feedback from attendees. Participants praised the comprehensive coverage of exam topics and the practical, actionable advice provided by Rohan. The certification process, which granted certificates of attendance to all participants, added a formal acknowledgment of their commitment to preparation. Overall, the GRE Exam Preparation Webinar successfully equipped attendees with the essential strategies and insights needed to excel in the GRE, underscoring the value of expert guidance in achieving their academic goals.
outcomes: The seminar provided participants with a solid understanding of the GRE exam structure and effective strategies for each section. Attendees gained practical problem-solving skills and techniques to manage their study schedules and test anxiety.

title: DUO’s DASH 2.0
description: As part of utsav24 BMSCE ACM Student Chapter had organized DUO’s DASH 2.0 which was a series of mini-games with a fun twist.
fullDescription:
Duo's Dash, a unique event designed to test teamwork and interdependence, saw an enthusiastic participation of 100 individuals paired into dynamic duos. Conducted on the 2nd floor common area of the PJ block, the event featured four innovative mini-games that challenged participants to race against the clock while demonstrating their cooperative skills. The atmosphere was charged with excitement and anticipation as teams navigated through a blend of AI-driven and traditional game modes, vying for the title of the most reciprocal and dynamic duo.

The event kicked off with "Inverted Pictionary," where one teammate drew a word on an inverted laptop screen, and the other guessed the object. This game tested not only their artistic skills but also their ability to synchronize hand and brain movements under pressure.

In "Movie Charades using AI," participants described movie scenes to an AI generator, which created images for their teammates to guess. This game highlighted the intersection of technology and creativity, pushing participants to think quickly and communicate effectively.

"Bomb Defuse" added a layer of tension, as duos had to verbally collaborate to defuse a virtual bomb in a Roblox game, with one holding the bomb and the other the manual. The intensity of this game required precise communication and trust, as any misstep could lead to a virtual explosion.

Finally, "Snake and Ladder" combined elements of chance and skill, as rolling dice led participants to perform fun activities, earning points for their teams. Each activity required participants to think on their feet and adapt quickly, adding to the overall excitement of the event.

As the day concluded, Duo's Dash celebrated the resilience and cooperation of its participants, with the undefeated pair against the clock emerging victorious. The event was praised for its innovative integration of AI and traditional gaming elements, providing a thrilling yet challenging experience. Participants shared their positive feedback, applauding the event's creativity and the seamless execution of the mini-games. Duo's Dash not only fostered teamwork and patience but also showcased the potential of AI in enhancing interactive and engaging activities, making it a memorable event for all involved.
outcomes: Overall it was a fun and engaging game, an all-round experience involving thrill,team-work and skill

title: GRANDMASTER’S GAMBIT 2.0
description: A chess variant competition organized by the BMSCE ACM Student Chapter in collaboration with Videsh Educon.
fullDescription: The GRANDMASTER’S GAMBIT 2.0, organized by the BMSCE ACM Student Chapter in collaboration with Videsh Educon, was a day-long competition focused on various chess variants, held on Friday, 17th May 2024, at the AIML Lab 1. The event ran from 9:00 AM to 3:30 PM. Initial rounds featured six pools, each with four to five teams, playing 4-player chess variants (4P, Chaturaji, and 4P Giveaway). The top two teams from each pool advanced. Round 2 introduced 2-player variations, including 'fog of war,' Atomic, Giveaway, and Crazy House. The semifinals were a round-robin format for the top teams, featuring unique variations like the XXL Variant (expanded chessboard) and Bughouse (simultaneous play on two adjacent boards with piece passing). A sudden-death 1v1 game served as the tie-breaker. The thrilling finals were a standard 4-player game, with the winning team receiving ₹2500 and the runners-up receiving ₹1500. The event successfully fostered connections and reinforced the love for chess among participants.
outcomes: The competition successfully showcased strategic prowess and sportsmanship, providing participants with valuable experiences and reinforcing the enduring appeal of chess.

title: CIPHER QUEST
description: A cryptography and puzzle-solving event where 30 teams decoded encrypted messages and solved intricate challenges to save their homeland.
fullDescription: The CIPHER QUEST event, organized by the BMSCE ACM Student Chapter, was held on Sunday, 19th May 2024, at MEL LAB 2. The event challenged 30 teams (two participants each) to decode encrypted messages contained within password-protected zip files. Teams earned 50 points per successfully unlocked folder by deciphering various types of codes. Additionally, they were required to demonstrate typing accuracy by blind typing sentences after each successful decipher. The elimination rounds involved decoding messages using different cipher techniques, testing cryptographic skills, teamwork, and communication under pressure. The teams with the fastest and most accurate decoding and typing advanced to the final round. The final round featured a unique game called Cryptic Ludo, which used two dice and allowed teams to use basic mathematical operations on the numbers rolled for strategy. Progressing through each quarter of the board required solving additional puzzles like Sudoku, crosswords, and mazes. Four teams reached the final showdown, demonstrating exceptional problem-solving, teamwork, and strategic thinking.
outcomes: The event successfully enhanced participants’ teamwork, problem-solving skills, and ability to think strategically and quickly under pressure.

title: InnoVision: Process and Prototype Design Workshop
description: An insightful seminar on process and prototype design, featuring Mr. Nithin Ravi, focusing on real-world applications and startup case studies.
image: InnoVision: Process and Prototype Design Workshop
fullDescription: The BMSCE ACM Student Chapter organized the InnoVision seminar on process and prototype design, featuring Mr. Nithin Ravi, an MBA candidate from ISB with substantial startup experience. Held in the ISE Seminar Hall, the session began with Mr. Ravi sharing his entrepreneurial background and insights into startup challenges. The core discussion utilized case studies of successful startups like Blinkit and Zepto to illustrate key principles of process design, emphasizing the importance of identifying and meeting the target audience's needs. Participants then engaged in a hands-on activity centered around a hypothetical startup called 'Fit Raho' (functional training). The task involved creating a complete process design to launch the offering and acquire the first 500 paid members for a new fitness center in Bangalore. This exercise required developing strategies for target audience, segmentation, targeting, positioning (STP), pricing, promotion, and location preferences. The combination of theoretical knowledge, practical case studies, and collaborative problem-solving made the seminar an informative and highly engaging experience.
outcomes: The seminar fostered an engaging and collaborative environment, enhancing participants' problem-solving skills and their ability to devise effective business strategies for process and prototype design.

title: CTRL+ALT+CREATE: Web Design Challenge
description: A four-round web design and coding competition focused on enhancing skills in HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, with a theme of Electric Vehicles (EVs).
fullDescription: The CTRL+ALT+CREATE web design challenge, hosted by the BMSCE ACM Student Chapter in collaboration with IEEE PES and SENSORS COUNCIL, aimed to enhance participants' skills in HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Held offline on 14th June 2024, the competition challenged teams of two to create static web pages based on the theme of Electric Vehicles (EVs) for PES Day. The event was structured into four rounds (from 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM), each with specific tasks, time limits, and points weighting. Tasks included designing a sticky navigation bar, implementing image and text transitions, creating accordion menus, and developing detailed footer sections. Participants were evaluated on creativity, adherence to guidelines, and the functionality of their designs. The team with the highest points at the end was declared the winner. The event successfully fostered technical proficiency, teamwork, and innovation.
outcomes: CTRL+ALT+CREATE was highly successful, showcasing participants' enhanced proficiency in web development fundamentals, creativity in design, and problem-solving abilities under time constraints. The event also fostered teamwork and collaboration among students interested in web design.
location: PG Lab 2, PJA Block 4th Floor, Dept of CSE

title: Byte Battle: Coding Competition
description: A multi-round coding competition and quiz event aimed at fostering teamwork, enhancing coding skills, and promoting competitive programming among students.
fullDescription:
The Byte Battle competition, organized by the BMSCE ACM Student Chapter, was a well-planned event held on June 20, 2024, in the PG Block, 2nd floor, Computer Lab, from 2:00 pm to 4:00 pm. The event promoted teamwork and coding skills among 17 teams (51 participants total). The competition consisted of multiple rounds: 

Byte Battle Challenge: Teams of three worked together, with each member solving one coding problem, testing problem-solving and collaboration. 
 Watts of Wisdom Round:A quiz segment on technological innovation and the future of electrification, testing broader technological understanding. 
Advanced Code Relay Challenge: Teams tackled more difficult coding problems by taking turns coding in short intervals, emphasizing adaptability and seamless collaboration under pressure. 

Overall, the dynamic and engaging event provided a valuable opportunity for participants to sharpen their coding skills, learn new strategies, and work effectively in teams.
outcomes: The Byte Battle provided participants with an excellent opportunity to enhance their coding skills, collaborate effectively in teams, and develop strategic thinking. The competitive environment and structured challenges prepared participants for real-world coding tasks and competitive programming events.
location: PG Block, 2nd floor, Computer Lab, BMSCE

title: Game Theory Showdown
description: An intellectually stimulating event focused on strategic thinking and decision-making, featuring classic and complex game theory-based challenges.
fullDescription:
The Game Theory Showdown, an intellectually stimulating event, engaged 25 participants in strategic thinking and decision-making through three challenging rounds. The event, hosted at PJ Block CR 507, aimed to test strategic thinking and decision-making skills in a fun and competitive environment, focusing on the application of game theory. 

Round 1: Featured classic games like 'Matching Pennies' and 'Dynamic Rock-Paper-Scissors-Lizard-Spock (RPSLS) with Cards,' introducing mixed strategies and Nash Equilibrium.
Round 2:Advanced to more complex scenarios with 'Sealed Bid Auctions' and 'Bertrand Competition,' emphasizing strategic bidding, pricing, and balancing competition with profit maximization. 
Final Round:Culminated in the intense 'Prisoner's Dilemma,' which tested participants' ability to build trust and strategize whether to cooperate or betray without knowing the other's choice. 

Participants showcased their logical reasoning, strategic planning, and adaptability, leaving with a deeper understanding of game theory principles.
outcomes: The event successfully enhanced participants' understanding of game theory concepts, fostered a spirit of healthy competition, and provided a platform for the practical application of strategic thinking, resulting in improved logical reasoning, strategic planning, and adaptability.

title: The Art of Encryption
description: An online poster design competition held during Cybersecurity Month with the theme "Secure Our World," aiming to raise awareness about digital safety.
fullDescription: The Art of Encryption was a poster-making competition held online throughout October 2024 by the BMSCE ACM Student Chapter to observe Cybersecurity Month. The theme was 'Secure Our World,' encouraging students to use a creative outlet to depict various cybersecurity concepts and raise digital safety awareness. Participants were given one month to design and submit their posters to a specified email, adhering to guidelines regarding format, size, and the inclusion of the BMSCE and BMSCE ACM Student Chapter logos. The posters were judged based on their relevance to the theme, creativity, quality of design, and clarity of message. The two best posters were selected, and the winners received a total prize pool of ₹1500/-.
outcomes: The event achieved tremendous success with 27 total participants. It provided a platform for participants to showcase their poster-making skills and creativity, successfully increasing awareness among them about the importance of cybersecurity and digital safety.

title: Jumble Jam 2.0
description: A fun-infused technical event consisting of three rounds designed to foster teamwork, creativity, and problem-solving skills among participants.
fullDescription:
The 'Jumble Jam 2.0' competition, organized by the BMSCE ACM Student Chapter in collaboration with BMSCE IEEE WIE, was a fun and challenging event held on October 28, 2024 (11:00 am to 1:00 pm). The event, held in the PJA Block, 6th floor, Computer Science Data Science Lab, was designed to test knowledge, technical skills, and teamwork among 27 teams (54 participants) through three rounds: 

 Round 1: Quiz Round - Teams of two answered 20 on-screen questions to test general knowledge. 
 Round 2: Tech and Fun - Teammates split roles; one solved technical questions while the other played online games. Performance in this round earned letters. 
 Round 3: Unscramble - Teams reunited to use their earned letters to collaboratively unscramble a 9-letter word, relying on logical deduction. 

The team with the highest score across all rounds was declared the winner. The event successfully promoted critical thinking, collaboration, and competitive spirit.
outcomes: Jumble Jam 2.0 provided an excellent opportunity for participants to practice problem-solving, teamwork, and creative thinking in an engaging environment. The varied rounds ensured that each team member could contribute their strengths, promoting a collaborative spirit among participants.
location: PJA Block, 6th floor, Computer Science Data Science Lab, BMSCE

title: AI Frontiers Workshop
description: A 6-day comprehensive workshop and 36-hour hackathon focused on Generative AI, Large Language Models (LLMs), and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
fullDescription:
The 'AI Frontiers' workshop, conducted in collaboration with Hi-Devs, was a comprehensive 6-day event held primarily at the PJA Block, 7th floor, AIML Seminar Hall. The workshop aimed to equip 82 participants with foundational and advanced knowledge in Generative AI, LLMs, and RAG. 

 Days 1 & 2 (Nov 14-15): Focused on theoretical foundations, real-life applications, LLM types, token limits, and vector databases. 
 Day 3 (Nov 16): A hands-on coding session where participants implemented Generative AI using LangChain and LLMs. 
 Days 4 & 5 (Nov 17-18): A continuous 36-hour online hackathon involving 16-17 teams (2-4 members each) solving innovative problem statements related to Generative AI and RAG, avoiding reliance on conversational AI tools. 
 Day 6 (Nov 19): Project demos, presentations, and the announcement of winners. Prizes included ₹3,000 (1st), ₹2,000 (2nd), and ₹1,000 (3rd). 

The event successfully blended theory, practice, and competition, creating a holistic learning experience.
outcomes: The workshop secured 82 registrations, providing practical exposure that enhanced participants' problem-solving and collaborative skills. Eight attendees successfully secured internships with Hi-Devs, and the event fostered significant awareness of the transformative potential of Generative AI and RAG.
location: PJA Block, 7th floor, AIML Seminar Hall, BMSCE

title: Clock of Confusion
description: A fun and challenging event featuring a series of games designed to test participants' memory, riddle-solving, critical thinking, and teamwork skills.
fullDescription:
The 'Clock of Confusion' event, organized by the BMSCE ACM Student Chapter in collaboration with BMSCE IEEE Student Branch, was a fun and challenging event held on November 15, 2024 (2:00 pm to 4:00 pm) in PJ Block, 5th floor, classrooms 504 and 505. The event featured a series of engaging games for 23 teams (46 participants) designed to test critical thinking, teamwork, and strategy: 

 Puzzle Game: Fastest team to solve a scrambled puzzle with a reference image earned the most points. 
 Card Game: Teams arranged a scrambled deck of cards (1 to 10) in ascending order in the shortest time, restarting if they picked the wrong card. 
 Memory Game: Teams observed a table of objects for one minute and answered questions based on memory. 
 Trading Game: Teams traded random items to accumulate the most valuable combination, testing negotiation skills. 
 Mystery Box Conundrum: Teams strategically decided when to open boxes containing clues or challenges to manage their points. 

The team with the highest score at the end of all rounds was declared the winner, emphasizing the importance of strategy and quick decision-making.
outcomes: Clock of Confusion provided an excellent opportunity for participants to solve riddles in a new way, enhance teamwork, and practice creative thinking in a supportive and engaging environment. The varied rounds ensured that each team member could contribute their strengths, promoting a collaborative spirit.
location: PJ Block, 5th floor, classroom 504 and 505, BMSCE

title: ACM Nexus
description: A lively and engaging orientation meet organized to introduce new members to the club's activities, roles, and details of the ACM India Chapter Summit 2024.
fullDescription: ACM Nexus was a lively and engaging meet for new and existing members of the BMSCE ACM Student Chapter, held on November 22, 2024, from 4 PM to 6 PM in Audi 2 (PJA Block). The event began with a welcome by Srikanth, followed by Roshini discussing the club's recent and upcoming events. Kanishka provided a clear understanding of the various club roles and behind-the-scenes operations, including marketing, design, logistics, and sponsorship. Bhuvan then gave a detailed presentation on the ACM India Chapter Summit 2024, covering logistics, accommodation, tentative schedule, and introducing speakers. The atmosphere was kept fun and interactive with a quick game and a dedicated games session hosted by Indraneel and Devaj. The event concluded with a thank-you note by Hrithik and a group photo.
outcomes: ACM Nexus successfully connected members, clarified the different roles within the club, and provided detailed updates on future events, including the planning and logistics of the ACM India Chapter Summit 2024. The event left attendees feeling more involved and updated.

title: Web Wars 2.0: Sustainability Web Development Challenge
description: A 3-day frontend web development competition during Phaseshift 2024, challenging participants to design sustainability-focused web solutions using modern frameworks.
image: Web Wars 2.0: Sustainability Web Development Challenge
fullDescription:
Web Wars 2.0 was a 3-day tech adventure organized by the BMSCE ACM Student Chapter as part of the technical fest, PhaseShift 2024, themed 'Resurgence.' The competition challenged 37 participants (in teams of 2) to craft sustainability-focused websites. The event was held in CR1, 7th Floor, AIML Dept, PJ Block, and featured Mr. Deepak Chawla, Co-Founder of HiDevs, as a resource person.

 Day 0 (Dec 3): Theme Unveiling, focused on 'Resurgence' sub-themes like Green Tech, Eco-friendly Agriculture, and Sustainable Fashion.
 Day 1 (Dec 4): Four challenges were introduced, including designing a Navigation Bar, integrating Multimedia/Social Media, implementing a Dark Mode Toggle, and a Surprise Challenge: Carbon Footprint Calculator.
 Day 2 (Dec 5): The final day involved offline website presentations and evaluation, followed by judge guidance. Allowed technologies included HTML5/CSS3, JavaScript, React.js, Vue.js, Angular, SASS/LESS, WebGL, Bootstrap, and Tailwind CSS. The event successfully fostered creativity and skill enhancement.
outcomes: All participating teams successfully demonstrated their innovation and creativity skills. The event provided valuable skill enhancement and exposure to participants, concluding successfully with a competitive and enjoyable spirit.
location: CR1, 7TH FLOOR, AIML DEPT, PJ BLOCK, BMSCE

title: FLAG HEIST 2.0
description: A time-bound, competitive event centered on capturing multiple bases via Google Forms, testing participants' mathematical, visualization, and problem-solving skills.
fullDescription: FLAG HEIST 2.0, conducted by the BMSCE ACM Student Chapter on the occasion of Phase Shift 2024, challenged participants to capture multiple bases within a time limit of one hour and fifteen minutes. Bases were represented by Google Forms containing a series of questions across diverse categories, including tech-based, coding-based, Phase Shift theme-based, logical reasoning, quantitative analysis, mini-games, riddles, and case-study tech-based problems. Teams had to answer all questions correctly in a form to capture a base; even one wrong answer invalidated the capture. Points were awarded based on speed of submission: the first team to capture a base won a blue flag (50 points), the second won a pink flag (30 points), and subsequent captures won yellow flags (10 points). The event took place from 10 am to 1 pm in the CSBS Lab, PJA Block.
outcomes: The event successfully created an engaging and competitive atmosphere, promoting critical thinking, collaboration, and healthy competition. Participants had a chance to showcase their skills and expand their knowledge base.
location: CSBS Lab, 6th Floor, Dept of CSBS, PJA Block, BMSCE

title: ACM India Chapter Summit 2024: Sustainable Computing
description: A three-day national summit hosted by BMSCE and RVCE, uniting ACM Chapters across India to explore the theme of "Sustainable Computing" and foster collaboration.
image: ACM India Chapter Summit 2024: Sustainable Computing
fullDescription:
The ACM India Chapter Summit 2024 was a three-day national event (Dec 19-21) hosted by B.M.S. College of Engineering (BMSCE) and R.V. College of Engineering (RVCE), centered on the theme 'Sustainable Computing.' The summit united ACM Student Chapters, Professional Chapters, and SIG Chapters from all over India, focusing on community building and societal contributions. The commitment to sustainability was visible in all aspects, from eco-friendly materials used for merchandise to plastic-free banners.

 Key Highlights:

 Day 0 (Dec 19): Included participant check-ins, an address by Ms. Maria Dsouza Choudhary on the ACM Ambassador Program, and the networking activity 'Duos Dash,' concluding with a welcome dinner.
 Day 1 (Dec 20): Featured an Indian cultural welcome dance by BMSCE Paramvah, a talk by Dr. Meenakshi D’Souza (President, ACM India), and a keynote by Dr. Rama Govindaraju (NVIDIA) on 'Sustainable AI.' Award-winning chapters presented their best practices. The afternoon included a session at RVCE on 'Technology for Sustainability' and a poster competition on 'Imagining India in 2030 (SDG).'
 Day 2 (Dec 21): Featured keynotes on 'Online Education in the NEP Era' (Prof. Andrew Thangaraj, IIT Madras), 'Democratizing Data and Responsible AI' (Prof. Manish Parashar, University of Utah), and 'Technology for Social Good' (Raghu Dharmaraju, ARTPARK at IISc). Participants engaged in regional brainstorming sessions and peer group presentations to develop action plans for the upcoming year, with winners receiving ABF funding.

 Key Achievements:

 Seamless collaboration between BMSCE and RVCE.
 Inspired innovative discussions on Sustainable Computing.
 Fostered collaboration among 87 ACM Chapters across India.
outcomes: The summit successfully showcased technology's vital role in sustainability, encouraged innovative ideas and practical solutions, and created a collaborative space for knowledge-sharing and networking among students, faculty, and industry leaders.

title: Fun with Algorithms Workshop
description: An engaging workshop, led by Mr. Sachin Lodha (Chief Scientist, TCS Research), connecting theoretical algorithms to real-life problem-solving and decision-making under uncertainty.
fullDescription:
The 'Fun with Algorithms' workshop, led by Mr. Sachin Lodha, Chief Scientist at TCS Research, successfully connected theoretical algorithms with relatable, real-life problem-solving. The session started by comparing an algorithm to a 'good recipe'—a systematic, quick process that solves a problem in a limited number of steps. 

 Key Concepts and Problems Discussed:

 Hamlet’s Ski Rental Problem: This classic scenario introduced the concept of online algorithms and making optimal decisions under uncertainty (deciding whether to rent or buy skis daily without knowing the total duration). 
 Treasure Hunt Problem: This challenge involved a blindfolded person finding a treasure on the X-axis with the least walking. This demonstrated the doubling strategy and key ideas like competitive ratio and the Beeza-Yates theorem (a core principle in competitive analysis).

The workshop's objectives were achieved through engaging storytelling and practical examples, successfully sparking interest in algorithm design and competitive programming.
outcomes: The workshop provided participants with a clear understanding of algorithms and how to apply them to real-life problems. Participants learned practical strategies, such as the Doubling Strategy, and key ideas like online algorithms, competitive ratio, and cost optimization, fostering logical thinking and an interest in competitive programming.

title: GRANDMASTER’S GAMBIT 3.0: Team Chess Tournament
description: A thrilling, team-based online chess tournament on Chess.com, emphasizing strategic collaboration, adaptability, and joint decision-making across blitz, bullet, and rapid formats.
fullDescription: Grandmaster’s Gambit 3.0 was a thrilling and fast-paced online team chess tournament hosted on Chess.com. The unique event, held from 9:00 am to 4:00 pm, focused on team-based play, with each team consisting of two players combining their skills. The tournament featured a series of matches played in multiple chess formats, including blitz, bullet, and rapid games, challenging the teams' adaptability and versatility. Success hinged on effective communication, coordinated attacks, and synchronization between teammates. The team with the most overall victories across all games and formats was declared the champion, celebrating both strategic excellence and cooperative gameplay.
outcomes: The event successfully delivered an engaging and challenging experience, showcasing the value of strategic collaboration and adaptability under pressure. Participants walked away with enriched skills in communication, rapid decision-making, and tactical planning, strengthening the sense of community among chess enthusiasts.

title: Duo’s Dash 3.0
description: A fast-paced, fun-filled event for teams of two, held during UTSAV 2025, featuring mini-games that tested communication, creativity, and quick thinking.
fullDescription:
Duos Dash 3.0, organized by the BMSCE ACM Student Chapter during UTSAV 2025, was a fast-paced and fun-filled event for teams of two, held in the 2nd Floor Common Area of the PJA Block. The event, which had 46 registrations, featured four unique rounds:

 Bomb Diffusion (Roblox): Tested communication and trust, with one teammate giving instructions to the other to diffuse a virtual bomb.
 Fliptionary: Tested creativity and coordination, where one teammate drew in the air using a flipped screen, and the other guessed the word.
 Movie Charades (AI): One teammate gave a prompt to Google Gemini AI, and the other guessed the movie based on the AI-generated image.
 Draw & Dare (UNO): Used UNO cards to assign fun, color-based dares, earning teams points.

The event successfully blended tech, creativity, and teamwork, matching the high energy of UTSAV 2025.
outcomes: The games fostered a sense of connection, strengthened bonds between participants, and enhanced teamwork and initiative within the BMSCE ACM Student Chapter.
location: 2nd Floor Common Area, PJA Block, BMSCE

title: Clocked Out
description: A three-round competition organized for Utsav to challenge participants across multiple dimensions of strategy, memory, reflex, and logic through mini-games, trading, and riddle-solving.
fullDescription:
Clocked Out was a fun and challenging three-round competition held on account of Utsav in the PJA Block Common Area. It was designed to test critical thinking, smart planning, and puzzle-solving skills.

 Rounds:

 Round 1: Point Acquisition – Mini-Game Marathon: Teams earned points through three mini-games: ChronoSort (card arrangement), Nerf Nexus (dart game with point-doubling risk), and Picture It (memory recall). Earned points were used later for clues or sabotage.
 Round 2: The Barter of Relics: A card trading game using fictional, high-fantasy themed cards with hidden values. Players had to use strategy and negotiation to trade, with the scores (and who advanced) decided only after the card values were revealed.
 Round 3: The Final Convergence: A riddle-solving race where teams faced a series of connected riddles based on deep themes. Solving riddles required spending points, and teams could use special power-ups to gain an edge or delay rivals. The event culminated in a thrilling sprint to solve the final mystery first.
outcomes: The event successfully enhanced participants' skills in strategy, memory, logic, and teamwork. It promoted critical thinking and creativity, setting a solid base for future interactive activities that blend entertainment with thoughtful challenges.
location: 2nd Floor PJA Block, Common Area, BMSCE

title: 15 Days of Code
description: A 15-day online coding challenge on HackerRank designed to encourage coding consistency, enhance algorithmic thinking, and build problem-solving resilience.
fullDescription: The '15 Days of Code' was a successful online coding challenge hosted on the HackerRank platform, starting on April 15, 2025. The event aimed to promote regular practice and competitive coding skills among 285 registered participants, with 150 remaining as active coders. Participants solved one problem each day, starting with easy levels and gradually progressing to more challenging problems. A live leaderboard fostered a competitive spirit, motivating consistent effort. The top three participants were awarded prizes: the leader received a course of their choice (up to ₹700), and the 2nd and 3rd place winners received free registration to the ACM AI Agents Workshop.
outcomes: The event successfully fostered a culture of consistent coding practice. Participants developed their problem-solving abilities, learned new algorithms, and enhanced their coding efficiency. The competitive spirit motivated participants to push their limits.

title: ALGOTHON
description: A competitive three-round event designed to challenge and sharpen participants' algorithmic problem-solving, coding skills, and application of data structures.
fullDescription: Algothon was a competitive, three-round event focused on algorithmic problem-solving, held on April 15, 2025, from 11:00 am to 1:00 pm in the SMV Hall PG Block. Participants were presented with a series of questions or challenges requiring the application of algorithms, data structures, and programming skills. In each round, teams worked to solve the problems, earning 10 points for every question answered correctly. The main goal was to accumulate the highest score by correctly solving the maximum number of problems within the designated time limit for each round.
outcomes: The event successfully challenged and sharpened participants' algorithmic problem-solving and coding skills. It fostered logical thinking, efficient coding, and provided a platform for learning, networking, and sparking interest in computer science.

title: Eminent Speaker Program: CryptoVerse
description: An online talk by Mr. Sikhar Patranabis (Staff Research Scientist, IBM Research India) on the challenges of transforming theoretical cryptographic security into real-world applications, focusing on Zero Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs).
fullDescription: The Eminent Speaker Talk, 'CryptoVerse: Securing The Digital World,' was conducted online by Mr. Sikhar Patranabis, Staff Research Scientist at IBM Research India. The talk focused on bridging the gap between provable cryptographic security and real-world deployment against modern cyber threats. The central topic was Zero Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs), a cryptographic protocol allowing a prover to convince a verifier that a statement is true without revealing any information beyond the truth of the statement. ZKPs were explained via four core principles: completeness, soundness, zero knowledge, and succinctness, using intuitive examples like the color blindness test and the Ali Baba cave scenario. The session also detailed ZKPs of knowledge, the use of arithmetic circuits for complex relations, and modern protocols like SNARKs. Practical applications in blockchain technology, particularly zk-rollups, were highlighted for improving scalability and reducing gas fees.
outcomes: Participants gained valuable insights into applying theoretical cryptographic concepts to real-world systems, enhancing their understanding of advanced security protocols and encouraging exploration of research opportunities in cryptography and blockchain scalability.

title: Town Hall for ACM Students
description: A virtual event that united ACM student chapter members and faculty with the ACM India Council to foster collaboration, recognize achievements, and provide guidance for chapter growth.
fullDescription:
The Town Hall for ACM Students was a virtual event held on April 5, 2025, bringing together 125 attendees from ACM student chapters and faculty across the country for an enriching interaction with the ACM India Council. The session featured welcomes from ACM India Council members Dr. Yogesh Simmhan and Maria Dsouza Choudhary, and Dr. Seemantini K (BMSCE Faculty Sponsor).

 Key Sessions:

 Awards Recognition: Dr. Ranga Rajagopal recognized innovative contributions from student chapters.
 AMA Session (Ask Me Anything): Students interacted directly with ACM leaders (including Dr. Ponnurangam Kumaraguru and others) on topics like research careers, funding, and the chapter ecosystem.
 Region-wise Presentations: Chapters from all six ACM India regions showcased their key activities, challenges, and successes, emphasizing research outreach, collaboration, and social impact.
 Special Sessions: Included guidance on Activity-Based Funding (ABF) from Dr. Rutvi Shah and insights on 'How to Win Awards?' from Dr. Geetanjali Kale.
outcomes: The event fostered collaboration and provided clear guidance, encouraging student chapters to maintain consistency, enhance community engagement, and align with ACM’s core mission. It highlighted that proactive planning, regular documentation, and social media presence significantly improve a chapter’s visibility and potential for national awards.

title: ACM Student Chapter Annual General Meeting 2025
description: The annual meeting to review the chapter's achievements, present financial reports, recognize outgoing leaders, and introduce the newly elected student leadership team for 2025-26.
image: ACM Student Chapter Annual General Meeting 2025
fullDescription: The Annual General Meeting (AGM) 2025 was held on March 20th in Auditorium - 2 to review the chapter's past year and set the vision for the future. The event began with a welcome speech and the traditional lighting of the lamp by dignitaries. The Faculty Core (Dr. Gowrishankar, Faculty Advisor, and Dr. Seemanthini K, Faculty Sponsor) were introduced and acknowledged. Outgoing Office Bearers were recognized with mementos and certificates after delivering their annual reports: Harshavardhan presented the Secretary's report, Srujana provided an overview of annual achievements and growth, and Bhuvan presented the financial report. The newly appointed Office Bearers (Junior Coordinators) were officially welcomed by Meghana. The Chairperson for the 2025-26 tenure addressed the gathering, outlining the new vision and goals. The event concluded with valuable guidance from the Faculty Sponsor and a vote of thanks.
outcomes: The AGM successfully reviewed past progress, formally welcomed the new leaders, and established a stronger vision for the next year based on feedback and suggestions from the faculty and students.

title: SAMANVAYA #1 - Together for Change
description: A community service initiative where members organized a donation drive to provide lunch for 72 children at the Amrutha Shishu Nivasa orphanage in Bengaluru.
fullDescription: The BMSCE ACM Student Chapter initiated its community service journey with SAMANVAYA #1 on March 13, 2025. The event, themed 'Together for Change,' focused on providing lunch for 72 children at Amrutha Shishu Nivasa in Bengaluru. A total donation of ₹2,000 was made, comprising ₹1,000 from member contributions (14 donors) and ₹1,000 from the chapter’s treasury. Five members visited the orphanage to deliver the donation and spend time with the children. This initiative marked the beginning of the club’s commitment to community welfare, reinforcing the belief that collective efforts can create a lasting impact.
outcomes: The event successfully encouraged social responsibility and community engagement among members, fostered a sense of empathy, strengthened teamwork, and set a precedent for future community service initiatives under the SAMANVAYA series.
location: Amrutha Shishu Nivasa, NR Colony, Basavanagudi, Bengaluru

title: Trivi Arena
description: A tech-based trivia and puzzle competition, organized to challenge participants’ technical knowledge, problem-solving skills, and ability to think quickly across three engaging rounds.
fullDescription:
Trivi Arena was a fun, yet intellectually stimulating, three-round competition held on March 17, 2025 (11:00 am to 1:00 pm) in the PJ Block, 6th floor, Computer Science Data Science Lab. The event, organized for 25 teams (50 participants), tested technical knowledge, puzzles, and interactive gameplay.

 Rounds:

 Round 1: Quiz (10 mins): Teams answered 20 on-screen questions within 30 seconds per question, challenging their technical problem-solving abilities.
 Round 2: Tech and Fun (30 mins): This blended technical problem-solving with interactive gameplay. One teammate solved 12 technical questions while the other completed 3 online games. Both earned letters based on their performance.
 Round 3: Unscramble (10 mins): Teams reunited to use their earned letters to collaboratively unscramble a 9-letter word using teamwork and logical reasoning.

 The team with the highest score at the end of all three rounds was declared the winner.
outcomes: Trivi Arena provided an excellent opportunity for participants to sharpen their problem-solving, teamwork, and creative thinking abilities in a dynamic and encouraging atmosphere, promoting a strong sense of unity and cooperative effort.
location: PJ Block, 6th floor, Computer Science Data Science Lab, BMSCE

title: iTeach: CS Pathshala Program
description: A community service and technical education initiative, in collaboration with the BMSCE Rotaract Club, focused on introducing computational thinking concepts to school students through grade-specific, hands-on activities.
fullDescription:
The CS Pathshala program by the ACM India Council was integrated into the iTeach initiative by the BMSCE Rotaract Club, held at Vidya Vinayaka Samsthe, Bengaluru. The partnership introduced computational thinking through dynamic, grade-specific activities emphasizing problem-solving, logical reasoning, and teamwork.

 Activities by Grade:

 Grades 1 & 2: Focused on Pattern Parade (sequencing/categorization) and Sorting Station (logical thinking).
 Grades 3 & 4: Focused on Step-by-Step Instructions, Loops, and Decision-Making using a Battleship-style game and flowchart logic.
 Grades 5 & 6: Focused on Problem Decomposition and Data Representation via a Treasure Hunt using coordinates and riddles.
 Grade 7: Focused on Advanced Algorithms and Programming Concepts by having students write, test, and debug algorithms for creating a paper airplane, and using real-life scenarios to create decision trees (if-else conditions).
outcomes: The event successfully introduced computational thinking to young learners, fostering critical thinking and problem-solving skills among students, while volunteers honed essential skills like communication and teamwork.
